title: deparia auriculata var. auriculata
intro: Deparia auriculata var. auriculata, also known as the ear-leaved lady fern, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a medicinal plant traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, where it is valued for its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Deparia auriculata var. auriculata can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
